title: Badhri Narayanan temple
tagline: Badhri Narayanan temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Badri Narayanan temple (Tamil: பத்ரி நாராயணன் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple in the Madurai District in the entrance of Alagar Koil in the state of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Badri Narayanan temple (Tamil: பத்ரி நாராயணன் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple in the Madurai District in the entrance of Alagar Koil in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is built in the style of the Badrinath temple, Uttarakhand. Balaji is in a seated position in this temple. An idol of Adi Shankaracharya — an important Hindu saint in 8th century AD — is placed alongside of the temple.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kallalagar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kallalagar Temple or Kallazhagar Temple, also known by its own toponym Thirumaliruncholai, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Vishnu in Alagar Koyil, a village in Madurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Tamil Style of Architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Azhvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshiped as Kallalagar, and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumagal.

topPlaces.name: Alagar Kovil
topPlaces.description: Alagar Kovil is a village in Madurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village is centered around Kallalagar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Murugan Temple, Pazhamudircholai
topPlaces.description: Solaimalai Murugan Temple is a Hindu temple, located atop a hill, in the outskirts of Madurai in Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to Murugan, and is one of the six abodes of Murugan (Arupadaiveedu).

topPlaces.name: Mangulam
topPlaces.description: Mangulam or Mankulam is a village in Madurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Madurai.

topPlaces.name: Arumbanur
topPlaces.description: Arumbanur is a census town in Madurai-North, Madurai district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is 4.5 by road northeast of the village of Kadachanendal.

topPlaces.name: Kannadendal
topPlaces.description: Kannadendal is a census town in Madurai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Vellalapatti
topPlaces.description: A.Vallalapatti is a panchayat town in Melur taluk of Madurai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the nine panchayat towns in the district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Badhri Narayanan temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Badhri Narayanan temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Badhri Narayanan temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Badhri Narayanan temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Badhri Narayanan temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Badhri Narayanan temple?
faq.a: Stays in Badhri Narayanan temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Badhri Narayanan temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kallalagar Temple, Alagar Kovil, Murugan Temple, Pazhamudircholai, Mangulam, Arumbanur.
